title: Django Models
content:
### Defining Models

Models are Python classes that define the structure of your database tables. Each model maps to a single database table.

```python
from django.db import models
from django.utils import timezone

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    slug = models.SlugField(unique=True)
    content = models.TextField()
    published_date = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    is_published = models.BooleanField(default=False)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-published_date']
        verbose_name_plural = "Posts"

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title
```

### Common Field Types

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `CharField(max_length=...)` | Short to medium-length strings |
| `TextField()` | Large text content |
| `IntegerField()` | Integer values |
| `FloatField()` | Floating-point numbers |
| `DecimalField(max_digits=5, decimal_places=2)` | Precise decimal numbers |
| `BooleanField()` | True/False |
| `DateField()` / `DateTimeField()` | Dates and datetimes |
| `EmailField()` | Email addresses (validates format) |
| `URLField()` | URLs |
| `FileField(upload_to='files/')` / `ImageField(upload_to='images/')` | File uploads |
| `SlugField()` | URL-friendly labels (letters, numbers, hyphens) |
| `UUIDField()` | UUID primary keys |
| `JSONField()` | JSON data (PostgreSQL, MySQL 8+) |

### Field Options

```python
class Product(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)    # no duplicates
    price = models.DecimalField(max_digits=8, decimal_places=2, default=0.00)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True, null=True)   # optional field
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)     # set once on creation
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)         # updated on every save
```

### Meta Class

The inner `Meta` class controls model metadata:

```python
class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    author = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    pages = models.IntegerField()

    class Meta:
        db_table = 'library_books'        # custom table name
        ordering = ['title']              # default ordering
        verbose_name_plural = 'Books'
        unique_together = ['title', 'author']  # composite unique constraint
        indexes = [                       # database indexes
            models.Index(fields=['author']),
        ]
```

### Relationships

**ForeignKey (Many-to-One):**

```python
class Author(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class Book(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    author = models.ForeignKey(Author, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='books')
```

**ManyToManyField:**

```python
class Student(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class Course(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    students = models.ManyToManyField(Student, related_name='courses')
```

**OneToOneField:**

```python
class UserProfile(models.Model):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    bio = models.TextField(blank=True)
    avatar = models.ImageField(upload_to='avatars/')
```

### `on_delete` Options

- `CASCADE`: Delete related objects too
- `PROTECT`: Prevent deletion if related objects exist (raises `ProtectedError`)
- `SET_NULL`: Set the foreign key to NULL (requires `null=True`)
- `SET_DEFAULT`: Set to the default value
- `DO_NOTHING`: Leave it alone (database integrity may fail)

### Model Methods

```python
class Order(models.Model):
    items = models.ManyToManyField(Item)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def total_price(self):
        return sum(item.price for item in self.items.all())

    def mark_as_shipped(self):
        self.status = 'shipped'
        self.save()
```

### QuerySet API

```python
# Create
Post.objects.create(title="Hello", content="World")

# Read
Post.objects.all()
Post.objects.get(id=1)
Post.objects.filter(is_published=True)
Post.objects.exclude(author="draft")
Post.objects.filter(title__icontains="django")  # case-insensitive contains

# Update
post = Post.objects.get(id=1)
post.title = "Updated Title"
post.save()
Post.objects.filter(is_published=False).update(is_published=True)

# Delete
post.delete()
Post.objects.filter(is_published=False).delete()

# Chaining & Ordering
Post.objects.filter(is_published=True).order_by('-created_at')[:5]

# Aggregation
from django.db.models import Count, Avg
Author.objects.annotate(num_books=Count('books'))
Book.objects.aggregate(avg_price=Avg('price'))
```

quiz.question: Which field type would you use to establish a many-to-one relationship between models?
What does on_delete=models.CASCADE do?
options: Prevents deletion of the related object
Sets the foreign key to NULL
Deletes related objects when the referenced object is deleted
